heading: ## Falsifiability
body:
The following numeric thresholds are defined for falsification of the core hypothesis (χ_Ze(young) > χ_Ze(old)):
- Effect size δ = 0.05 (minimum detectable difference in χ_Ze)
- Significance level α = 0.001 (Bonferroni-corrected for multiple comparisons)
- Minimum effect size of interest d ≥ 0.5
- Statistical power ≥ 0.95
- Minimum AUC ≥ 0.75 for ROC analysis
- Total required sample size N ≥ 161 (placeholder, to be updated after pilot data; see Sample size calculation)
If the observed effect falls below these thresholds, the hypothesis is considered falsified.

heading: ## Pre-registration plan
body: The study will be pre-registered on the Open Science Framework (OSF) prior to data collection. The placeholder registration ID is osf.io/TBD. The planned pre-registration date is 2026-12-31. The pre-registration will include the primary hypothesis, study design, sample size justification, analysis plan, and all outcome measures.

heading: ## Sample size calculation
body:
Sample size is calculated using the formula for a two-sample t-test (two-tailed):

n = (Z_α/2 + Z_β)² · 2σ² / δ²

Where:
- Z_α/2 = 1.96 (for α = 0.05, two-tailed)
- Z_β = 0.84 (for power = 0.80)
- σ = pooled standard deviation (placeholder: TBD from pilot data)
- δ = minimum detectable difference in χ_Ze (placeholder: TBD)

For the HRV module, the estimated per-group N is 161 (placeholder). For the EEG module, the estimated per-group N is 110 (placeholder). These values are preliminary and will be updated after pilot data collection. The final N will be reported in the pre-registration.

heading: ## Risk matrix
body:
| # | Risk | Probability | Impact | Mitigation |
|---|------|-------------|--------|------------|
| 1 | Low recruitment of elderly participants | Medium | High | Partner with geriatric clinics; offer compensation |
| 2 | Sensor noise in EEG recordings | Medium | Medium | Use shielded caps; implement real-time artifact rejection |
| 3 | Variability in olfactory stimulus delivery | High | Medium | Standardize odor concentration and delivery protocol |
| 4 | Data loss due to device malfunction | Low | High | Redundant data storage; daily device checks |
| 5 | Confounding by medication use | Medium | Medium | Exclude participants on CNS-active drugs; collect medication logs |
| 6 | Failure to replicate χ_Ze age effect | Low | High | Pre-register analysis plan; use multiple datasets for validation |

heading: ## Limitations
body:
The following limitations are acknowledged:
- The sample size calculation is based on placeholder values and will be updated after pilot data.
- The EEG validation datasets are not fully representative of all demographic groups (e.g., limited geographic diversity).
- The olfactory module is still in the design phase and has not been empirically validated.
- The HRV module relies on consumer-grade wearable sensors, which may have lower accuracy than clinical-grade devices.
- The Ze theory parameter v* has two formulations (Python form and Article form); cross-subproject consistency is not yet fully established.
- The study does not control for all potential confounders (e.g., sleep quality, circadian rhythm).
- The placebo effect cannot be fully excluded in the olfactory diagnostic component.
- Long-term longitudinal data are not available to assess within-subject stability of χ_Ze.
- The consortium partners are listed as placeholders and may change during the project.

heading: ## Consortium / partners
body:
The following partners are involved in the BioSense project (roles are placeholders):
- Partner A (placeholder): EEG hardware development and validation
- Partner B (placeholder): HRV sensor integration and data analysis
- Partner C (placeholder): Olfactory stimulus design and delivery
- Partner D (placeholder): Clinical recruitment and data collection
- Partner E (placeholder): Statistical analysis and pre-registration support
Formal agreements and detailed roles will be finalized in the project kick-off meeting.
